revision.text:
[[en:Kohori Kofun Cluster]][[ja:古保利古墳群]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q85877692]] Wikidata name: Kohori Kofun Cluster [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:36, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q85877692]] Wikidata name: Kohori Kofun Cluster [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun and Nara period bural mound in Nagahama, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name =Kohori Kofun Cluster | native_name = 古保利古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Shiga Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kohori Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|28|41.83|N|136|dd|36.2|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location =[[:en:Nagahama, Shiga|Nagahama, Shiga]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]; {{ill|Nagahama|WD=Q844130}}| region = [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] | type = [[Kofun]] cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 3rd to 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Kohori Kofun Cluster'''|古保利古墳群||}} is a group of [[Kofun period|Kofun]] to early [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Takatsuki neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Nagahama, Shiga|Nagahama]], [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2003.{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/164561|title=古保利古墳群|trans-title= Kohori Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}} It is the largest ''kofun'' cluster in Shiga Prefecture. ==Overview== The Kohori Kofun Cluster extends along a three kilometer ridge running north-to-south on Nishinoyama hill facing Shiotsu Bay at the northern end of [[:en:Lake Biwa|Lake Biwa]]. The ridge has an elevation of 200 to 240 meters, and is 100 meters above the surface of Lake Biwa. The cluster consists of seven keyhole-shaped {{nihongo|''[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, eight "two conjoined rectangles" type ({{ill|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because of the mass replacement error that gets rid of the links like this otherwise|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}), one {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|lt=scallop-shaped|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} {{nihongo|''hotategai-gata-fun''|帆立貝型墳}}, 79 {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) and 37 {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square-type|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}) for a total of 132 tumuli.{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=43dd750404}}{{in lang|ja}} These have been divided by archaeologists into six groupings labelled "Group A" through "Group F" during a survey in 1965. The largest is the Nishinoyama Kofun, which has a total length of 90 meters. Most of the circular and square mounds are from 10 to 20 meters in diameter or on each side, with the largest at no more the 30 meters. The larger ''[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]'' and ''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}'' are estimated to date from the early Kofun period, or around the beginning of the 3rd century AD. This has been verified by an [[archaeological excavation|excavation]] of the Komatsu Kofun from 1998 to 2000, from which numerous [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] were recovered, including much earthenware, [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s and copper and iron products. No ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' have been recovered from any of the tumuli in the cluster, but ''[[fukiishi]]'' have been found on some of the earlier tombs. The smaller circular and square tombs date to as late as the early 7th century AD, or into the early [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]]. The site is unusual not only in the number of tumuli, but in the variety of styles. In addition, although it is on the major highway from the Yamato region to the [[:en:Sea of Japan|Sea of Japan]], the ''kofun'' are visible only from Lake Biwa, indicating that the builders had a strong connection to water transportation on the lake. The site is about ten minutes by car from [[:en:Takatsuki Station (Shiga)|Takatsuki Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Hokuriku Main Line|Hokuriku Main Line]]. {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:90%" |+Kobori Kofun Cluster main tumuli ! rowspan = 2 | Branch ! colspan = 2 | Kofun name ! rowspan = 2 | Coordinates ! rowspan = 2 | Shape ! rowspan = 2 | Scale ! rowspan = 2 | Construction time ! rowspan = 2 | Remarks |- !Branch # !Common name |- |rowspan=4|A||A-1||'''深谷古墳'''||{{coord|35|28|45.08|N|136|dd|27.90|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Kohori A-1（Fukaya Kofun）}}||''zenpō-kōen-fun''|| || || |- |A-2||'''西野山古墳'''||{{coord|35|28|41.83|N|136|dd|36.20|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark||display=inline|name=古保利A-2号墳（西野山古墳）}}||''zenpō-kōen-fun''||length 90m|| ||style="text-align:left"|largest in cluster |- |A-3||'''A-3号墳'''|| ||''enpun''||dia 15m||late Kofun period||style="text-align:left"|Confirmation survey |- |A-5||'''巳ノ脇古墳'''|| || || || || |- |rowspan=4|B||B-1||'''ヘソ岩古墳'''||{{coord|35|28|25.94|N|136|dd|40.26|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline|name=古保利B-1号墳（ヘソ岩古墳）}}||''hotategai-gata''||length 21m|| || |- |B-6||'''小松古墳'''||{{coord|35|28|20.82|N|136|dd|38.95|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline|name=古保利B-6号墳（小松古墳）}}||''zenpō-kōhō-fun''|||length 60m||early Kofun period||style="text-align:left"|oldest in cluster

[[en:Kohori Kofun Cluster]][[ja:古保利古墳群]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q85877692]] Wikidata name: Kohori Kofun Cluster [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:36, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q85877692]] Wikidata name: Kohori Kofun Cluster [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun and Nara period bural mound in Nagahama, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name =Kohori Kofun Cluster | native_name = 古保利古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Shiga Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kohori Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|28|41.83|N|136|dd|36.2|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location =[[:en:Nagahama, Shiga|Nagahama, Shiga]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]; {{ill|Nagahama|WD=Q844130}}| region = [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] | type = [[Kofun]] cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 3rd to 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Kohori Kofun Cluster'''|古保利古墳群||}} is a group of [[Kofun period|Kofun]] to early [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Takatsuki neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Nagahama, Shiga|Nagahama]], [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2003.{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/164561|title=古保利古墳群|trans-title= Kohori Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}} It is the largest ''kofun'' cluster in Shiga Prefecture. ==Overview== The Kohori Kofun Cluster extends along a three kilometer ridge running north-to-south on Nishinoyama hill facing Shiotsu Bay at the northern end of [[:en:Lake Biwa|Lake Biwa]]. The ridge has an elevation of 200 to 240 meters, and is 100 meters above the surface of Lake Biwa. The cluster consists of seven keyhole-shaped {{nihongo|''[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, eight "two conjoined rectangles" type ({{ill|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because of the mass replacement error that gets rid of the links like this otherwise|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}), one {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|lt=scallop-shaped|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} {{nihongo|''hotategai-gata-fun''|帆立貝型墳}}, 79 {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) and 37 {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square-type|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}) for a total of 132 tumuli.{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=43dd750404}}{{in lang|ja}} These have been divided by archaeologists into six groupings labelled "Group A" through "Group F" during a survey in 1965. The largest is the Nishinoyama Kofun, which has a total length of 90 meters. Most of the circular and square mounds are from 10 to 20 meters in diameter or on each side, with the largest at no more the 30 meters. The larger ''[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]'' and ''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}'' are estimated to date from the early Kofun period, or around the beginning of the 3rd century AD. This has been verified by an [[archaeological excavation|excavation]] of the Komatsu Kofun from 1998 to 2000, from which numerous [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] were recovered, including much earthenware, [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s and copper and iron products. No ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' have been recovered from any of the tumuli in the cluster, but ''[[fukiishi]]'' have been found on some of the earlier tombs. The smaller circular and square tombs date to as late as the early 7th century AD, or into the early [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]]. The site is unusual not only in the number of tumuli, but in the variety of styles. In addition, although it is on the major highway from the Yamato region to the [[:en:Sea of Japan|Sea of Japan]], the ''kofun'' are visible only from Lake Biwa, indicating that the builders had a strong connection to water transportation on the lake. The site is about ten minutes by car from [[:en:Takatsuki Station (Shiga)|Takatsuki Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Hokuriku Main Line|Hokuriku Main Line]]. {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:90%" |+Kobori Kofun Cluster main tumuli ! rowspan = 2 | Branch ! colspan = 2 | Kofun name ! rowspan = 2 | Coordinates ! rowspan = 2 | Shape ! rowspan = 2 | Scale ! rowspan = 2 | Construction time ! rowspan = 2 | Remarks |- !Branch # !Common name |- |rowspan=4|A||A-1||'''深谷古墳'''||{{coord|35|28|45.08|N|136|dd|27.90|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Kohori A-1（Fukaya Kofun）}}||''zenpō-kōen-fun''|| || || |- |A-2||'''西野山古墳'''||{{coord|35|28|41.83|N|136|dd|36.20|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark||display=inline|name=古保利A-2号墳（西野山古墳）}}||''zenpō-kōen-fun''||length 90m|| ||style="text-align:left"|largest in cluster |- |A-3||'''A-3号墳'''|| ||''enpun''||dia 15m||late Kofun period||style="text-align:left"|Confirmation survey |- |A-5||'''巳ノ脇古墳'''|| || || || || |- |rowspan=4|B||B-1||'''ヘソ岩古墳'''||{{coord|35|28|25.94|N|136|dd|40.26|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline|name=古保利B-1号墳（ヘソ岩古墳）}}||''hotategai-gata''||length 21m|| || |- |B-6||'''小松古墳'''||{{coord|35|28|20.82|N|136|dd|38.95|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline|name=古保利B-6号墳（小松古墳）}}||''zenpō-kōhō-fun''|||length 60m||early Kofun period||style="text-align:left"|oldest in cluster

[[en:Kohori Kofun Cluster]][[ja:古保利古墳群]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q85877692]] Wikidata name: Kohori Kofun Cluster [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:36, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q85877692]] Wikidata name: Kohori Kofun Cluster [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun and Nara period bural mound in Nagahama, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name =Kohori Kofun Cluster | native_name = 古保利古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Shiga Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kohori Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|28|41.83|N|136|dd|36.2|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location =[[:en:Nagahama, Shiga|Nagahama, Shiga]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]; {{ill|Nagahama|WD=Q844130}}| region = [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] | type = [[Kofun]] cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 3rd to 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Kohori Kofun Cluster'''|古保利古墳群||}} is a group of [[Kofun period|Kofun]] to early [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Takatsuki neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Nagahama, Shiga|Nagahama]], [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2003.{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/164561|title=古保利古墳群|trans-title= Kohori Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}} It is the largest ''kofun'' cluster in Shiga Prefecture. ==Overview== The Kohori Kofun Cluster extends along a three kilometer ridge running north-to-south on Nishinoyama hill facing Shiotsu Bay at the northern end of [[:en:Lake Biwa|Lake Biwa]]. The ridge has an elevation of 200 to 240 meters, and is 100 meters above the surface of Lake Biwa. The cluster consists of seven keyhole-shaped {{nihongo|''[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, eight "two conjoined rectangles" type ({{ill|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because of the mass replacement error that gets rid of the links like this otherwise|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}), one {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|lt=scallop-shaped|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} {{nihongo|''hotategai-gata-fun''|帆立貝型墳}}, 79 {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) and 37 {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square-type|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}) for a total of 132 tumuli.{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=43dd750404}}{{in lang|ja}} These have been divided by archaeologists into six groupings labelled "Group A" through "Group F" during a survey in 1965. The largest is the Nishinoyama Kofun, which has a total length of 90 meters. Most of the circular and square mounds are from 10 to 20 meters in diameter or on each side, with the largest at no more the 30 meters. The larger ''[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]'' and ''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}'' are estimated to date from the early Kofun period, or around the beginning of the 3rd century AD. This has been verified by an [[archaeological excavation|excavation]] of the Komatsu Kofun from 1998 to 2000, from which numerous [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] were recovered, including much earthenware, [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s and copper and iron products. No ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' have been recovered from any of the tumuli in the cluster, but ''[[fukiishi]]'' have been found on some of the earlier tombs. The smaller circular and square tombs date to as late as the early 7th century AD, or into the early [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]]. The site is unusual not only in the number of tumuli, but in the variety of styles. In addition, although it is on the major highway from the Yamato region to the [[:en:Sea of Japan|Sea of Japan]], the ''kofun'' are visible only from Lake Biwa, indicating that the builders had a strong connection to water transportation on the lake. The site is about ten minutes by car from [[:en:Takatsuki Station (Shiga)|Takatsuki Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Hokuriku Main Line|Hokuriku Main Line]]. {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:90%" |+Kobori Kofun Cluster main tumuli ! rowspan = 2 | Branch ! colspan = 2 | Kofun name ! rowspan = 2 | Coordinates ! rowspan = 2 | Shape ! rowspan = 2 | Scale ! rowspan = 2 | Construction time ! rowspan = 2 | Remarks |- !Branch # !Common name |- |rowspan=4|A||A-1||'''深谷古墳'''||{{coord|35|28|45.08|N|136|dd|27.90|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Kohori A-1（Fukaya Kofun）}}||''zenpō-kōen-fun''|| || || |- |A-2||'''西野山古墳'''||{{coord|35|28|41.83|N|136|dd|36.20|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark||display=inline|name=古保利A-2号墳（西野山古墳）}}||''zenpō-kōen-fun''||length 90m|| ||style="text-align:left"|largest in cluster |- |A-3||'''A-3号墳'''|| ||''enpun''||dia 15m||late Kofun period||style="text-align:left"|Confirmation survey |- |A-5||'''巳ノ脇古墳'''|| || || || || |- |rowspan=4|B||B-1||'''ヘソ岩古墳'''||{{coord|35|28|25.94|N|136|dd|40.26|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline|name=古保利B-1号墳（ヘソ岩古墳）}}||''hotategai-gata''||length 21m|| || |- |B-6||'''小松古墳'''||{{coord|35|28|20.82|N|136|dd|38.95|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline|name=古保利B-6号墳（小松古墳）}}||''zenpō-kōhō-fun''|||length 60m||early Kofun period||style="text-align:left"|oldest in cluster

kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}), one {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|lt=scallop-shaped|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} {{nihongo|''hotategai-gata-fun''|帆立貝型墳}}, 79 {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) and 37 {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square-type|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}) for a total of 132 tumuli.{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=43dd750404}}{{in lang|ja}} These have been divided by archaeologists into six groupings labelled "Group A" through "Group F" during a survey in 1965. The largest is the Nishinoyama Kofun, which has a total length of 90 meters. Most of the circular and square mounds are from 10 to 20 meters in diameter or on each side, with the largest at no more the 30 meters. The larger ''[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]'' and ''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}'' are estimated to date from the early Kofun period, or around the beginning of the 3rd century AD. This has been verified by an [[archaeological excavation|excavation]] of the Komatsu Kofun from 1998 to 2000, from which numerous [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] were recovered, including much earthenware, [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s and copper and iron products. No ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' have been recovered from any of the tumuli in the cluster, but ''[[fukiishi]]'' have been found on some of the earlier tombs. The smaller circular and square tombs date to as late as the early 7th century AD, or into the early [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]]. The site is unusual not only in the number of tumuli, but in the variety of styles. In addition, although it is on the major highway from the Yamato region to the [[:en:Sea of Japan|Sea of Japan]], the ''kofun'' are visible only from Lake Biwa, indicating that the builders had a strong connection to water transportation on the lake. The site is about ten minutes by car from [[:en:Takatsuki Station (Shiga)|Takatsuki Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Hokuriku Main Line|Hokuriku Main Line]]. {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:90%" |+Kobori Kofun Cluster main tumuli ! rowspan = 2 | Branch ! colspan = 2 | Kofun name ! rowspan = 2 | Coordinates ! rowspan = 2 | Shape ! rowspan = 2 | Scale ! rowspan = 2 | Construction time ! rowspan = 2 | Remarks |- !Branch # !Common name |- |rowspan=4|A||A-1||'''深谷古墳'''||{{coord|35|28|45.08|N|136|dd|27.90|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Kohori A-1（Fukaya Kofun）}}||''zenpō-kōen-fun''|| || || |- |A-2||'''西野山古墳'''||{{coord|35|28|41.83|N|136|dd|36.20|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark||display=inline|name=古保利A-2号墳（西野山古墳）}}||''zenpō-kōen-fun''||length 90m|| ||style="text-align:left"|largest in cluster |- |A-3||'''A-3号墳'''|| ||''enpun''||dia 15m||late Kofun period||style="text-align:left"|Confirmation survey |- |A-5||'''巳ノ脇古墳'''|| || || || || |- |rowspan=4|B||B-1||'''ヘソ岩古墳'''||{{coord|35|28|25.94|N|136|dd|40.26|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline|name=古保利B-1号墳（ヘソ岩古墳）}}||''hotategai-gata''||length 21m|| || |- |B-6||'''小松古墳'''||{{coord|35|28|20.82|N|136|dd|38.95|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline|name=古保利B-6号墳（小松古墳）}}||''zenpō-kōhō-fun''|||length 60m||early Kofun period||style="text-align:left"|oldest in cluster

revision.text:
[[en:Kohori Kofun Cluster]][[ja:古保利古墳群]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q85877692]] Wikidata name: Kohori Kofun Cluster [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:36, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q85877692]] Wikidata name: Kohori Kofun Cluster [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun and Nara period bural mound in Nagahama, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name =Kohori Kofun Cluster | native_name = 古保利古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Shiga Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kohori Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|28|41.83|N|136|dd|36.2|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location =[[:en:Nagahama, Shiga|Nagahama, Shiga]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]; {{ill|Nagahama|WD=Q844130}}| region = [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] | type = [[Kofun]] cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 3rd to 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Kohori Kofun Cluster'''|古保利古墳群||}} is a group of [[Kofun period|Kofun]] to early [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Takatsuki neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Nagahama, Shiga|Nagahama]], [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2003.{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/164561|title=古保利古墳群|trans-title= Kohori Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}} It is the largest ''kofun'' cluster in Shiga Prefecture. ==Overview== The Kohori Kofun Cluster extends along a three kilometer ridge running north-to-south on Nishinoyama hill facing Shiotsu Bay at the northern end of [[:en:Lake Biwa|Lake Biwa]]. The ridge has an elevation of 200 to 240 meters, and is 100 meters above the surface of Lake Biwa. The cluster consists of seven keyhole-shaped {{nihongo|''[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, eight "two conjoined rectangles" type ({{ill|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because of the mass replacement error that gets rid of the links like this otherwise|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}), one {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|lt=scallop-shaped|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} {{nihongo|''hotategai-gata-fun''|帆立貝型墳}}, 79 {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) and 37 {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square-type|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}) for a total of 132 tumuli.{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=43dd750404}}{{in lang|ja}} These have been divided by archaeologists into six groupings labelled "Group A" through "Group F" during a survey in 1965. The largest is the Nishinoyama Kofun, which has a total length of 90 meters. Most of the circular and square mounds are from 10 to 20 meters in diameter or on each side, with the largest at no more the 30 meters. The larger ''[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]'' and ''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}'' are estimated to date from the early Kofun period, or around the beginning of the 3rd century AD. This has been verified by an [[archaeological excavation|excavation]] of the Komatsu Kofun from 1998 to 2000, from which numerous [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] were recovered, including much earthenware, [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s and copper and iron products. No ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' have been recovered from any of the tumuli in the cluster, but ''[[fukiishi]]'' have been found on some of the earlier tombs. The smaller circular and square tombs date to as late as the early 7th century AD, or into the early [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]]. The site is unusual not only in the number of tumuli, but in the variety of styles. In addition, although it is on the major highway from the Yamato region to the [[:en:Sea of Japan|Sea of Japan]], the ''kofun'' are visible only from Lake Biwa, indicating that the builders had a strong connection to water transportation on the lake. The site is about ten minutes by car from [[:en:Takatsuki Station (Shiga)|Takatsuki Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Hokuriku Main Line|Hokuriku Main Line]]. {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:90%" |+Kobori Kofun Cluster main tumuli ! rowspan = 2 | Branch ! colspan = 2 | Kofun name ! rowspan = 2 | Coordinates ! rowspan = 2 | Shape ! rowspan = 2 | Scale ! rowspan = 2 | Construction time ! rowspan = 2 | Remarks |- !Branch # !Common name |- |rowspan=4|A||A-1||'''深谷古墳'''||{{coord|35|28|45.08|N|136|dd|27.90|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Kohori A-1（Fukaya Kofun）}}||''zenpō-kōen-fun''|| || || |- |A-2||'''西野山古墳'''||{{coord|35|28|41.83|N|136|dd|36.20|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark||display=inline|name=古保利A-2号墳（西野山古墳）}}||''zenpō-kōen-fun''||length 90m|| ||style="text-align:left"|largest in cluster |- |A-3||'''A-3号墳'''|| ||''enpun''||dia 15m||late Kofun period||style="text-align:left"|Confirmation survey |- |A-5||'''巳ノ脇古墳'''|| || || || || |- |rowspan=4|B||B-1||'''ヘソ岩古墳'''||{{coord|35|28|25.94|N|136|dd|40.26|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline|name=古保利B-1号墳（ヘソ岩古墳）}}||''hotategai-gata''||length 21m|| || |- |B-6||'''小松古墳'''||{{coord|35|28|20.82|N|136|dd|38.95|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline|name=古保利B-6号墳（小松古墳）}}||''zenpō-kōhō-fun''|||length 60m||early Kofun period||style="text-align:left"|oldest in cluster

[[en:Kohori Kofun Cluster]][[ja:古保利古墳群]] [[en:Kohori Kofun Cluster]][[ja:古保利古墳群]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q85877692]] Wikidata name: Kohori Kofun Cluster [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:36, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q85877692]] Wikidata name: Kohori Kofun Cluster [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun and Nara period bural mound in Nagahama, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name =Kohori Kofun Cluster | native_name = 古保利古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Shiga Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kohori Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|28|41.83|N|136|dd|36.2|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location =[[:en:Nagahama, Shiga|Nagahama, Shiga]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]; {{ill|Nagahama|WD=Q844130}}| region = [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] | type = [[Kofun]] cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 3rd to 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Kohori Kofun Cluster'''|古保利古墳群||}} is a group of [[Kofun period|Kofun]] to early [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Takatsuki neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Nagahama, Shiga|Nagahama]], [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2003.{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/164561|title=古保利古墳群|trans-title= Kohori Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}} It is the largest ''kofun'' cluster in Shiga Prefecture. ==Overview== The Kohori Kofun Cluster extends along a three kilometer ridge running north-to-south on Nishinoyama hill facing Shiotsu Bay at the northern end of [[:en:Lake Biwa|Lake Biwa]]. The ridge has an elevation of 200 to 240 meters, and is 100 meters above the surface of Lake Biwa. The cluster consists of seven keyhole-shaped {{nihongo|''[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, eight "two conjoined rectangles" type ({{ill|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because of the mass replacement error that gets rid of the links like this otherwise|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}), one {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|lt=scallop-shaped|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} {{nihongo|''hotategai-gata-fun''|帆立貝型墳}}, 79 {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) and 37 {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square-type|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}) for a total of 132 tumuli.{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=43dd750404}}{{in lang|ja}} These have been divided by archaeologists into six groupings labelled "Group A" through "Group F" during a survey in 1965. The largest is the Nishinoyama Kofun, which has a total length of 90 meters. Most of the circular and square mounds are from 10 to 20 meters in diameter or on each side, with the largest at no more the 30 meters. The larger ''[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]'' and ''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}'' are estimated to date from the early Kofun period, or around the beginning of the 3rd century AD. This has been verified by an [[archaeological excavation|excavation]] of the Komatsu Kofun from 1998 to 2000, from which numerous [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] were recovered, including much earthenware, [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s and copper and iron products. No ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' have been recovered from any of the tumuli in the cluster, but ''[[fukiishi]]'' have been found on some of the earlier tombs. The smaller circular and square tombs date to as late as the early 7th century AD, or into the early [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]]. The site is unusual not only in the number of tumuli, but in the variety of styles. In addition, although it is on the major highway from the Yamato region to the [[:en:Sea of Japan|Sea of Japan]], the ''kofun'' are visible only from Lake Biwa, indicating that the builders had a strong connection to water transportation on the lake. The site is about ten minutes by car from [[:en:Takatsuki Station (Shiga)|Takatsuki Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Hokuriku Main Line|Hokuriku Main Line]]. {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:90%" |+Kobori Kofun Cluster main tumuli ! rowspan = 2 | Branch ! colspan = 2 | Kofun name ! rowspan = 2 | Coordinates ! rowspan = 2 | Shape ! rowspan = 2 | Scale ! rowspan = 2 | Construction time ! rowspan = 2 | Remarks |- !Branch # !Common name |- |rowspan=4|A||A-1||'''深谷古墳'''||{{coord|35|28|45.08|N|136|dd|27.90|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Kohori A-1（Fukaya Kofun）}}||''zenpō-kōen-fun''|| || || |- |A-2||'''西野山古墳'''||{{coord|35|28|41.83|N|136|dd|36.20|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark||display=inline|name=古保利A-2号墳（西野山古墳）}}||''zenpō-kōen-fun''||length 90m|| ||style="text-align:left"|largest in cluster |- |A-3||'''A-3号墳'''|| ||''enpun''||dia 15m||late Kofun period||style="text-align:left"|Confirmation survey |- |A-5||'''巳ノ脇古墳'''|| || || || || |- |rowspan=4|B||B-1||'''ヘソ岩古墳'''||{{coord|35|28|25.94|N|136|dd|40.26|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline|name=古保利B-1号墳（ヘソ岩古墳）}}||''hotategai-gata''||length 21m|| || |- |B-6||'''小松古墳'''||{{coord|35|28|20.82|N|136|dd|38.95|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline|name=古保利B-6号墳（小松古墳）}}||''zenpō-kōhō-fun''|||length 60m||early Kofun period||style="text-align:left"|oldest in cluster

The site is unusual not only in the number of tumuli, but in the variety of styles. In addition, although it is on the major highway from the Yamato region to the [[:en:Sea of Japan|Sea of Japan]], the ''kofun'' are visible only from Lake Biwa, indicating that the builders had a strong connection to water transportation on the lake. [[en:Kohori Kofun Cluster]][[ja:古保利古墳群]] [[en:Kohori Kofun Cluster]][[ja:古保利古墳群]] [[en:Kohori Kofun Cluster]][[ja:古保利古墳群]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q85877692]] Wikidata name: Kohori Kofun Cluster [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:36, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q85877692]] Wikidata name: Kohori Kofun Cluster [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun and Nara period bural mound in Nagahama, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name =Kohori Kofun Cluster | native_name = 古保利古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Shiga Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kohori Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|28|41.83|N|136|dd|36.2|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location =[[:en:Nagahama, Shiga|Nagahama, Shiga]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]; {{ill|Nagahama|WD=Q844130}}| region = [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] | type = [[Kofun]] cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 3rd to 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Kohori Kofun Cluster'''|古保利古墳群||}} is a group of [[Kofun period|Kofun]] to early [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Takatsuki neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Nagahama, Shiga|Nagahama]], [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2003.{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/164561|title=古保利古墳群|trans-title= Kohori Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}} It is the largest ''kofun'' cluster in Shiga Prefecture. ==Overview== The Kohori Kofun Cluster extends along a three kilometer ridge running north-to-south on Nishinoyama hill facing Shiotsu Bay at the northern end of [[:en:Lake Biwa|Lake Biwa]]. The ridge has an elevation of 200 to 240 meters, and is 100 meters above the surface of Lake Biwa. The cluster consists of seven keyhole-shaped {{nihongo|''[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, eight "two conjoined rectangles" type ({{ill|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because of the mass replacement error that gets rid of the links like this otherwise|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}), one {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|lt=scallop-shaped|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} {{nihongo|''hotategai-gata-fun''|帆立貝型墳}}, 79 {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) and 37 {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square-type|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}) for a total of 132 tumuli.{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=43dd750404}}{{in lang|ja}} These have been divided by archaeologists into six groupings labelled "Group A" through "Group F" during a survey in 1965. The largest is the Nishinoyama Kofun, which has a total length of 90 meters. Most of the circular and square mounds are from 10 to 20 meters in diameter or on each side, with the largest at no more the 30 meters. The larger ''[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]'' and ''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}'' are estimated to date from the early Kofun period, or around the beginning of the 3rd century AD. This has been verified by an [[archaeological excavation|excavation]] of the Komatsu Kofun from 1998 to 2000, from which numerous [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] were recovered, including much earthenware, [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s and copper and iron products. No ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' have been recovered from any of the tumuli in the cluster, but ''[[fukiishi]]'' have been found on some of the earlier tombs. The smaller circular and square tombs date to as late as the early 7th century AD, or into the early [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]]. The site is unusual not only in the number of tumuli, but in the variety of styles. In addition, although it is on the major highway from the Yamato region to the [[:en:Sea of Japan|Sea of Japan]], the ''kofun'' are visible only from Lake Biwa, indicating that the builders had a strong connection to water transportation on the lake. The site is about ten minutes by car from [[:en:Takatsuki Station (Shiga)|Takatsuki Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Hokuriku Main Line|Hokuriku Main Line]]. {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:90%" |+Kobori Kofun Cluster main tumuli ! rowspan = 2 | Branch ! colspan = 2 | Kofun name ! rowspan = 2 | Coordinates ! rowspan = 2 | Shape ! rowspan = 2 | Scale ! rowspan = 2 | Construction time ! rowspan = 2 | Remarks |- !Branch # !Common name |- |rowspan=4|A||A-1||'''深谷古墳'''||{{coord|35|28|45.08|N|136|dd|27.90|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Kohori A-1（Fukaya Kofun）}}||''zenpō-kōen-fun''|| || || |- |A-2||'''西野山古墳'''||{{coord|35|28|41.83|N|136|dd|36.20|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark||display=inline|name=古保利A-2号墳（西野山古墳）}}||''zenpō-kōen-fun''||length 90m|| ||style="text-align:left"|largest in cluster |- |A-3||'''A-3号墳'''|| ||''enpun''||dia 15m||late Kofun period||style="text-align:left"|Confirmation survey |- |A-5||'''巳ノ脇古墳'''|| || || || || |- |rowspan=4|B||B-1||'''ヘソ岩古墳'''||{{coord|35|28|25.94|N|136|dd|40.26|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline|name=古保利B-1号墳（ヘソ岩古墳）}}||''hotategai-gata''||length 21m|| || |- |B-6||'''小松古墳'''||{{coord|35|28|20.82|N|136|dd|38.95|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline|name=古保利B-6号墳（小松古墳）}}||''zenpō-kōhō-fun''|||length 60m||early Kofun period||style="text-align:left"|oldest in cluster

{{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Kohori Kofun Cluster'''|古保利古墳群||}} is a group of [[Kofun period|Kofun]] to early [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Takatsuki neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Nagahama, Shiga|Nagahama]], [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2003.{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/164561|title=古保利古墳群|trans-title= Kohori Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}} It is the largest ''kofun'' cluster in Shiga Prefecture. ==Overview== The Kohori Kofun Cluster extends along a three kilometer ridge running north-to-south on Nishinoyama hill facing Shiotsu Bay at the northern end of [[:en:Lake Biwa|Lake Biwa]]. The ridge has an elevation of 200 to 240 meters, and is 100 meters above the surface of Lake Biwa. The cluster consists of seven keyhole-shaped {{nihongo|''[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, eight "two conjoined rectangles" type ({{ill|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because of the mass replacement error that gets rid of the links like this otherwise|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}), one {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|lt=scallop-shaped|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} {{nihongo|''hotategai-gata-fun''|帆立貝型墳}}, 79 {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) and 37 {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square-type|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}) for a total of 132 tumuli.{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=43dd750404}}{{in lang|ja}} These have been divided by archaeologists into six groupings labelled "Group A" through "Group F" during a survey in 1965. The largest is the Nishinoyama Kofun, which has a total length of 90 meters. Most of the circular and square mounds are from 10 to 20 meters in diameter or on each side, with the largest at no more the 30 meters. The larger ''[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]'' and ''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}'' are estimated to date from the early Kofun period, or around the beginning of the 3rd century AD. This has been verified by an [[archaeological excavation|excavation]] of the Komatsu Kofun from 1998 to 2000, from which numerous [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] were recovered, including much earthenware, [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s and copper and iron products. No ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' have been recovered from any of the tumuli in the cluster, but ''[[fukiishi]]'' have been found on some of the earlier tombs. The smaller circular and square tombs date to as late as the early 7th century AD, or into the early [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]]. The site is unusual not only in the number of tumuli, but in the variety of styles. In addition, although it is on the major highway from the Yamato region to the [[:en:Sea of Japan|Sea of Japan]], the ''kofun'' are visible only from Lake Biwa, indicating that the builders had a strong connection to water transportation on the lake. The site is about ten minutes by car from [[:en:Takatsuki Station (Shiga)|Takatsuki Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Hokuriku Main Line|Hokuriku Main Line]]. {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:90%" |+Kobori Kofun Cluster main tumuli ! rowspan = 2 | Branch ! colspan = 2 | Kofun name ! rowspan = 2 | Coordinates ! rowspan = 2 | Shape ! rowspan = 2 | Scale ! rowspan = 2 | Construction time ! rowspan = 2 | Remarks |- !Branch # !Common name |- |rowspan=4|A||A-1||'''深谷古墳'''||{{coord|35|28|45.08|N|136|dd|27.90|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Kohori A-1（Fukaya Kofun）}}||''zenpō-kōen-fun''|| || || |- |A-2||'''西野山古墳'''||{{coord|35|28|41.83|N|136|dd|36.20|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark||display=inline|name=古保利A-2号墳（西野山古墳）}}||''zenpō-kōen-fun''||length 90m|| ||style="text-align:left"|largest in cluster |- |A-3||'''A-3号墳'''|| ||''enpun''||dia 15m||late Kofun period||style="text-align:left"|Confirmation survey |- |A-5||'''巳ノ脇古墳'''|| || || || || |- |rowspan=4|B||B-1||'''ヘソ岩古墳'''||{{coord|35|28|25.94|N|136|dd|40.26|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline|name=古保利B-1号墳（ヘソ岩古墳）}}||''hotategai-gata''||length 21m|| || |- |B-6||'''小松古墳'''||{{coord|35|28|20.82|N|136|dd|38.95|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline|name=古保利B-6号墳（小松古墳）}}||''zenpō-kōhō-fun''|||length 60m||early Kofun period||style="text-align:left"|oldest in cluster

{{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Kohori Kofun Cluster'''|古保利古墳群||}} is a group of [[Kofun period|Kofun]] to early [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Takatsuki neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Nagahama, Shiga|Nagahama]], [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2003.{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/164561|title=古保利古墳群|trans-title= Kohori Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}} It is the largest ''kofun'' cluster in Shiga Prefecture. ==Overview== The Kohori Kofun Cluster extends along a three kilometer ridge running north-to-south on Nishinoyama hill facing Shiotsu Bay at the northern end of [[:en:Lake Biwa|Lake Biwa]]. The ridge has an elevation of 200 to 240 meters, and is 100 meters above the surface of Lake Biwa. The cluster consists of seven keyhole-shaped {{nihongo|''[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, eight "two conjoined rectangles" type kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}), one {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|lt=scallop-shaped|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} {{nihongo|''hotategai-gata-fun''|帆立貝型墳}}, 79 {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}) for a total of 132 tumuli.{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=43dd750404}}{{in lang|ja}} These have been divided by archaeologists into six groupings labelled "Group A" through "Group F" during a survey in 1965. The largest is the Nishinoyama Kofun, which has a total length of 90 meters. Most of the circular and square mounds are from 10 to 20 meters in diameter or on each side, with the largest at no more the 30 meters. The larger ''[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]'' and ''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}'' are estimated to date from the early Kofun period, or around the beginning of the 3rd century AD. This has been verified by an [[archaeological excavation|excavation]] of the Komatsu Kofun from 1998 to 2000, from which numerous [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] were recovered, including much earthenware, [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s and copper and iron products. No ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' have been recovered from any of the tumuli in the cluster, but ''[[fukiishi]]'' have been found on some of the earlier tombs. The smaller circular and square tombs date to as late as the early 7th century AD, or into the early [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]]. The site is unusual not only in the number of tumuli, but in the variety of styles. In addition, although it is on the major highway from the Yamato region to the [[:en:Sea of Japan|Sea of Japan]], the ''kofun'' are visible only from Lake Biwa, indicating that the builders had a strong connection to water transportation on the lake. The site is about ten minutes by car from [[:en:Takatsuki Station (Shiga)|Takatsuki Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Hokuriku Main Line|Hokuriku Main Line]]. {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:90%" |+Kobori Kofun Cluster main tumuli ! rowspan = 2 | Branch ! colspan = 2 | Kofun name ! rowspan = 2 | Coordinates ! rowspan = 2 | Shape ! rowspan = 2 | Scale ! rowspan = 2 | Construction time ! rowspan = 2 | Remarks |- !Branch # !Common name |- |rowspan=4|A||A-1||'''深谷古墳'''||{{coord|35|28|45.08|N|136|dd|27.90|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Kohori A-1（Fukaya Kofun）}}||''zenpō-kōen-fun''|| || || |- |A-2||'''西野山古墳'''||{{coord|35|28|41.83|N|136|dd|36.20|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark||display=inline|name=古保利A-2号墳（西野山古墳）}}||''zenpō-kōen-fun''||length 90m|| ||style="text-align:left"|largest in cluster |- |A-3||'''A-3号墳'''|| ||''enpun''||dia 15m||late Kofun period||style="text-align:left"|Confirmation survey |- |A-5||'''巳ノ脇古墳'''|| || || || || |- |rowspan=4|B||B-1||'''ヘソ岩古墳'''||{{coord|35|28|25.94|N|136|dd|40.26|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline|name=古保利B-1号墳（ヘソ岩古墳）}}||''hotategai-gata''||length 21m|| || |- |B-6||'''小松古墳'''||{{coord|35|28|20.82|N|136|dd|38.95|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline|name=古保利B-6号墳（小松古墳）}}||''zenpō-kōhō-fun''|||length 60m||early Kofun period||style="text-align:left"|oldest in cluster

[[en:Kohori Kofun Cluster]][[ja:古保利古墳群]] [[en:Kohori Kofun Cluster]][[ja:古保利古墳群]] [[en:Kohori Kofun Cluster]][[ja:古保利古墳群]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q85877692]] Wikidata name: Kohori Kofun Cluster [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:36, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q85877692]] Wikidata name: Kohori Kofun Cluster [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun and Nara period bural mound in Nagahama, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name =Kohori Kofun Cluster | native_name = 古保利古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Shiga Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kohori Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|28|41.83|N|136|dd|36.2|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location =[[:en:Nagahama, Shiga|Nagahama, Shiga]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]; {{ill|Nagahama|WD=Q844130}}| region = [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] | type = [[Kofun]] cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 3rd to 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Kohori Kofun Cluster'''|古保利古墳群||}} is a group of [[Kofun period|Kofun]] to early [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Takatsuki neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Nagahama, Shiga|Nagahama]], [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2003.{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/164561|title=古保利古墳群|trans-title= Kohori Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}} It is the largest ''kofun'' cluster in Shiga Prefecture. ==Overview== The Kohori Kofun Cluster extends along a three kilometer ridge running north-to-south on Nishinoyama hill facing Shiotsu Bay at the northern end of [[:en:Lake Biwa|Lake Biwa]]. The ridge has an elevation of 200 to 240 meters, and is 100 meters above the surface of Lake Biwa. The cluster consists of seven keyhole-shaped {{nihongo|''[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, eight "two conjoined rectangles" type ({{ill|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because of the mass replacement error that gets rid of the links like this otherwise|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}), one {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|lt=scallop-shaped|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} {{nihongo|''hotategai-gata-fun''|帆立貝型墳}}, 79 {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) and 37 {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square-type|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}) for a total of 132 tumuli.{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=43dd750404}}{{in lang|ja}} These have been divided by archaeologists into six groupings labelled "Group A" through "Group F" during a survey in 1965. The largest is the Nishinoyama Kofun, which has a total length of 90 meters. Most of the circular and square mounds are from 10 to 20 meters in diameter or on each side, with the largest at no more the 30 meters. The larger ''[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]'' and ''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}'' are estimated to date from the early Kofun period, or around the beginning of the 3rd century AD. This has been verified by an [[archaeological excavation|excavation]] of the Komatsu Kofun from 1998 to 2000, from which numerous [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] were recovered, including much earthenware, [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s and copper and iron products. No ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' have been recovered from any of the tumuli in the cluster, but ''[[fukiishi]]'' have been found on some of the earlier tombs. The smaller circular and square tombs date to as late as the early 7th century AD, or into the early [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]]. The site is unusual not only in the number of tumuli, but in the variety of styles. In addition, although it is on the major highway from the Yamato region to the [[:en:Sea of Japan|Sea of Japan]], the ''kofun'' are visible only from Lake Biwa, indicating that the builders had a strong connection to water transportation on the lake. The site is about ten minutes by car from [[:en:Takatsuki Station (Shiga)|Takatsuki Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Hokuriku Main Line|Hokuriku Main Line]]. {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:90%" |+Kobori Kofun Cluster main tumuli ! rowspan = 2 | Branch ! colspan = 2 | Kofun name ! rowspan = 2 | Coordinates ! rowspan = 2 | Shape ! rowspan = 2 | Scale ! rowspan = 2 | Construction time ! rowspan = 2 | Remarks |- !Branch # !Common name |- |rowspan=4|A||A-1||'''深谷古墳'''||{{coord|35|28|45.08|N|136|dd|27.90|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Kohori A-1（Fukaya Kofun）}}||''zenpō-kōen-fun''|| || || |- |A-2||'''西野山古墳'''||{{coord|35|28|41.83|N|136|dd|36.20|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark||display=inline|name=古保利A-2号墳（西野山古墳）}}||''zenpō-kōen-fun''||length 90m|| ||style="text-align:left"|largest in cluster |- |A-3||'''A-3号墳'''|| ||''enpun''||dia 15m||late Kofun period||style="text-align:left"|Confirmation survey |- |A-5||'''巳ノ脇古墳'''|| || || || || |- |rowspan=4|B||B-1||'''ヘソ岩古墳'''||{{coord|35|28|25.94|N|136|dd|40.26|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline|name=古保利B-1号墳（ヘソ岩古墳）}}||''hotategai-gata''||length 21m|| || |- |B-6||'''小松古墳'''||{{coord|35|28|20.82|N|136|dd|38.95|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline|name=古保利B-6号墳（小松古墳）}}||''zenpō-kōhō-fun''|||length 60m||early Kofun period||style="text-align:left"|oldest in cluster

kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}), one {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|lt=scallop-shaped|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} {{nihongo|''hotategai-gata-fun''|帆立貝型墳}}, 79 {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) and 37 {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square-type|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}) for a total of 132 tumuli.{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=43dd750404}}{{in lang|ja}} These have been divided by archaeologists into six groupings labelled "Group A" through "Group F" during a survey in 1965. The largest is the Nishinoyama Kofun, which has a total length of 90 meters. Most of the circular and square mounds are from 10 to 20 meters in diameter or on each side, with the largest at no more the 30 meters. The larger ''[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]'' and ''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}'' are estimated to date from the early Kofun period, or around the beginning of the 3rd century AD. This has been verified by an [[archaeological excavation|excavation]] of the Komatsu Kofun from 1998 to 2000, from which numerous [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] were recovered, including much earthenware, [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s and copper and iron products. No ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' have been recovered from any of the tumuli in the cluster, but ''[[fukiishi]]'' have been found on some of the earlier tombs. The smaller circular and square tombs date to as late as the early 7th century AD, or into the early [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]]. The site is unusual not only in the number of tumuli, but in the variety of styles. In addition, although it is on the major highway from the Yamato region to the [[:en:Sea of Japan|Sea of Japan]], the ''kofun'' are visible only from Lake Biwa, indicating that the builders had a strong connection to water transportation on the lake. The site is about ten minutes by car from [[:en:Takatsuki Station (Shiga)|Takatsuki Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Hokuriku Main Line|Hokuriku Main Line]]. {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:90%" |+Kobori Kofun Cluster main tumuli ! rowspan = 2 | Branch ! colspan = 2 | Kofun name ! rowspan = 2 | Coordinates ! rowspan = 2 | Shape ! rowspan = 2 | Scale ! rowspan = 2 | Construction time ! rowspan = 2 | Remarks |- !Branch # !Common name |- |rowspan=4|A||A-1||'''深谷古墳'''||{{coord|35|28|45.08|N|136|dd|27.90|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Kohori A-1（Fukaya Kofun）}}||''zenpō-kōen-fun''|| || || |- |A-2||'''西野山古墳'''||{{coord|35|28|41.83|N|136|dd|36.20|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark||display=inline|name=古保利A-2号墳（西野山古墳）}}||''zenpō-kōen-fun''||length 90m|| ||style="text-align:left"|largest in cluster |- |A-3||'''A-3号墳'''|| ||''enpun''||dia 15m||late Kofun period||style="text-align:left"|Confirmation survey |- |A-5||'''巳ノ脇古墳'''|| || || || || |- |rowspan=4|B||B-1||'''ヘソ岩古墳'''||{{coord|35|28|25.94|N|136|dd|40.26|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline|name=古保利B-1号墳（ヘソ岩古墳）}}||''hotategai-gata''||length 21m|| || |- |B-6||'''小松古墳'''||{{coord|35|28|20.82|N|136|dd|38.95|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline|name=古保利B-6号墳（小松古墳）}}||''zenpō-kōhō-fun''|||length 60m||early Kofun period||style="text-align:left"|oldest in cluster

{{ill|Nagahama|WD=Q844130}}| region = [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] | type = [[Kofun]] cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 3rd to 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Kohori Kofun Cluster'''|古保利古墳群||}} is a group of [[Kofun period|Kofun]] to early [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Takatsuki neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Nagahama, Shiga|Nagahama]], [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2003.{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/164561|title=古保利古墳群|trans-title= Kohori Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}} It is the largest ''kofun'' cluster in Shiga Prefecture. ==Overview== The Kohori Kofun Cluster extends along a three kilometer ridge running north-to-south on Nishinoyama hill facing Shiotsu Bay at the northern end of [[:en:Lake Biwa|Lake Biwa]]. The ridge has an elevation of 200 to 240 meters, and is 100 meters above the surface of Lake Biwa. The cluster consists of seven keyhole-shaped {{nihongo|''[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one
